text:
Oliver loved collecting rocks. Every trip outdoors was an opportunity to find new specimens. One autumn, a storm blew him away.

He missed his favorite place. One rainy afternoon he wandered down a seldom-used lane. There was an old oak tree—it must be centuries old!

He touched its bark. It felt heavy. Oliver climbed higher. Leaves tangled around his feet. The trunk was thick!

Finally he reached an enormous branch. Wow! An owl peeked down!

Owl hooted in greeting. Oliver hid.

Hours flew by. Owl hootsed again.

"The night is quiet."

* * *

Owl hootsed once more.

* * *

"The moon is big."*

* * *

Suddenly big gray wings spread out.

A nocturnal dragon!

They eyed each other.

"You're ugly."*

* * *

Oliver retreated.

"*You.**

* * *

A week passed. Oliver wouldn't go near the tree.

One day...

* ...

*The dragon's
